title: For investors with ability – and patience to wait 3 years, not 3 months: 6 large-cap stocks with upside potential of up to 33%
summary: The interesting point about the large-cap space today is not that prices have collapsed. In most cases, they have not. The more important change is quieter. Several established businesses are available at valuations that look more reasonable than they did 18 months ago. Their fundamentals have not broken. Their market positions have not disappeared. Their balance sheets have not deteriorated. That is what creates an opportunity.

title: Dearness Allowance: Teachers' bodies in West Bengal welcome 20% DA hike, want another increase this year — What we know
summary: West Bengal has announced a 20% hike in dearness allowance for state employees and pensioners, raising it to 38% effective October 1. Teachers' organizations welcomed the decision but urged faster implementation and clearance of remaining dues.

title: Mcap of 6 of top-10 most valued firms climbs Rs 88,678 cr; ICICI Bank biggest winner
summary: Indian stock markets saw a modest gain last week, with the top 10 companies adding Rs 88,678.1 crore in market valuation. ICICI Bank led the surge, followed by HDFC Bank and Reliance Industries. However, Bharti Airtel, TCS, and LIC experienced significant valuation drops. This positive market sentiment was attributed to easing crude oil prices and improved geopolitical developments.

title: Funding the 'mother of all cycles': Chris Wood cuts Indian stocks to double down on South Korean chip giants
summary: Christopher Wood has cut select Indian equities to increase exposure to South Korean chipmakers like SK Hynix and Samsung Electronics, betting on the AI-driven capex cycle. He views memory stocks as core beneficiaries, citing strong demand, attractive valuations and structural shifts, while warning that malinvestment risks could eventually disrupt the AI trade.

title: Passive investing explosion: DSP’s Anil Ghelani predicts ETFs, index funds will command 30% of mutual fund industry
summary: Passive investing is gaining rapid traction in India, with DSP’s Anil Ghelani projecting ETFs and index funds to grow from 17% to 30% of mutual fund assets within five years. The shift reflects changing investor behaviour, with passive strategies forming core portfolios and active funds playing a selective, high-alpha role.

title: The hidden reason global capital is looking beyond India for future growth
summary: Foreign investors are reallocating capital away from India towards markets like Taiwan and South Korea that offer stronger exposure to AI and semiconductor growth. The trend highlights India’s structural gaps in innovation, limited deep-tech opportunities in listed markets, and rising concerns over valuations amid moderating earnings and slowing capital inflows.

title: Will Sensex, Nifty extend gains or turn volatile? Q1 updates, F&O expiry among 8 factors set to steer stock market this week

title: Can I-T dept raise tax over alleged cash paid for property? ITAT clarifies
summary: The Mumbai ITAT held that the I-T Department cannot make a tax addition for an alleged cash payment in a property purchase based solely on documents seized from a third party. Such additions must be backed by independent and corroborative evidence linking the buyer to the transaction.
